description_long: This is an upright, weathered stone headstone with a rounded top and a decorative relief carving near the upper section, possibly depicting an angel or cherub. The front face features an inscribed panel, though the text is largely eroded and illegible, with some dark staining and moss growth near the base. The headstone sits on a rectangular stone plinth, which is also aged and partially covered with moss. The back of the headstone is plain, showing significant discoloration and weathering, with reddish-brown streaks. The sides are narrow and reveal the thickness of the stone, with some chipping and surface irregularities. This type of grave marker is commonly found in older cemeteries and urban graveyards, serving as a memorial for the deceased.
description: A weathered upright stone headstone with a rounded top, decorative relief, and eroded inscription, set on a mossy plinth.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed relief carving, inscribed panel, and visible weathering with moss at the base.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the headstone's thickness, narrow profile, and some surface chipping.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, heavily weathered surface with reddish-brown discoloration and no visible inscription.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing the stone's thickness and the plinth's mossy edge.
